    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/org/orgimpl/store.go", "symbol": "AddOrgUser"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/user_sync.go", "symbol": "SyncUserHook"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/user_sync.go", "symbol": "FetchSyncedUserHook"},

    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/ldap/api/service.go", "symbol": "PostSyncUserWithLDAP"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/rbac_sync.go", "symbol": "SyncPermissionsHook"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/access_control.go", "symbol": "Evaluate"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go", "symbol": "getUserTeams"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/queries.go", "symbol": "GetPermissions"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/api/folder.go", "symbol": "GetFolders"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go", "symbol": "GetInheritedScopes"},
    {"repo": "grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go", "symbol": "Get"}
  ],
  "text": "The organization/team/user permission hierarchy in Grafana is implemented across multiple interdependent services. Here's how they work together:\n\n## OrgUser Role Assignment\nThe core organization user management is defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` with the `OrgUser` struct representing an organization membership record. The assignment process is handled through:\n- `pkg/services/org/orgimpl/store.go` - Contains `InsertOrgUser`, `AddOrgUser`, and `UpdateOrgUser` functions that manage org user database operations\n- `pkg/api/org_users.go` - HTTP API endpoints for managing organization users and their roles\n\n## Team Management and Sync\nTeam functionality is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/team/model.go` - Defines `Team` and `TeamMember` structures\n- `pkg/services/team/teamimpl/team.go` - Team service implementation\n- `pkg/services/team/teamimpl/store.go` - Database operations including `AddOrUpdateTeamMemberHook` and `RemoveTeamMemberHook` for team membership changes\n- `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` - HTTP API for team member management with support for both manual and LDAP-synced members\n\n## Team/Group Synchronization from Identity Providers\nThe team synchronization from external identity providers is handled through:\n- `pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go` - Defines the `Groups` interface with `GetTeams()` method that maps LDAP groups to Grafana teams\n- `pkg/services/ldap/ldap.go` - LDAP user and group retrieval logic\n- `pkg/services/ldap/api/service.go` - API endpoint for syncing users with LDAP, including team membership\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/ldap.go` - LDAP authentication client that populates `Identity.Groups` from LDAP group memberships\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/oauth.go` - OAuth2 client that extracts groups from OAuth2 tokens\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/proxy.go` - Auth proxy client that reads groups from proxy headers\n\n## Identity Synchronization Hooks\nThe identity synchronization pipeline manages both user and team sync:\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/user_sync.go` - `SyncUserHook` and `FetchSyncedUserHook` coordinate user data sync and populate teams\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/org_sync.go` - `SyncOrgRolesHook` handles organization role assignment from identity providers\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/rbac_sync.go` - `SyncPermissionsHook` fetches and applies RBAC permissions\n- `pkg/services/authn/identity.go` - Core `Identity` struct with `Groups` field (IdP groups) and `Teams` field (assigned Grafana teams)\n- `pkg/services/authn/authn.go` - `ClientParams` struct defines `SyncTeams` and `SyncOrgRoles` flags\n\n## Folder Permission Inheritance\nFolder-level permissions are inherited by dashboards within them:\n- `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - `GetInheritedScopes()` function retrieves inherited permission scopes from parent folders\n- `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` - Folder service that checks inherited permissions during folder access\n- `pkg/api/folder.go` - HTTP API for folder management with permission evaluation\n- `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` - Folder permission endpoints\n\n## RBAC Fine-Grained Permission Evaluation\nThe RBAC permission evaluation engine is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` - `AccessControl` interface for permission checks and `Service` interface for role management\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` - `Evaluator` type that constructs permission evaluation expressions with AND/OR logic\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/access_control.go` - `Evaluate()` method performs actual permission checks against user permissions\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` - `Permission` (role-action-scope binding) and `ResourcePermission` models\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` - `SetUserResourcePermission()` and team/role permission management\n- `pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go` - `getUserTeams()` retrieves teams for a user and their inherited permissions\n- `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/queries.go` - Low-level SQL queries for permission resolution\n- `pkg/registry/apis/iam/resourcepermission/sql.go` - Resource permission SQL backend\n\n## Permission Flow\nWhen a user authenticates:\n1. Identity provider groups are extracted into `Identity.Groups`\n2. `SyncUserHook` creates/updates the user and `FetchSyncedUserHook` populates `Identity.Teams` with assigned teams\n3. `SyncOrgRolesHook` assigns org-level roles from IdP org role mappings\n4. `SyncPermissionsHook` loads user permissions via RBAC service\n5. `AccessControl.Evaluate()` checks permissions using action-scope pairs\n6. For folders/dashboards, `GetInheritedScopes()` includes parent folder permissions\n7. RBAC service resolves team memberships and applies role-based permissions\n\nThis hierarchy ensures that users inherit permissions through organizations, teams, and folder inheritance, while RBAC provides fine-grained control over individual actions on resources."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Organization and Team Permission Hierarchy

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the organization/team/user permission hierarchy: the OrgUser role assignment, the team group sync, the folder permission inheritance, and the RBAC fine-grained permission evaluation.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files implementing the organization/team/user permission hierarchy in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the grafana/grafana codebase and created the answer file at `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary

The analysis identified **41 Go source files** across Grafana that implement the organization/team/user permission hierarchy:

### Key Components:

1. **OrgUser Role Assignment** (`pkg/services/org/`)
   - Manages organization membership and role assignment

2. **Team Management** (`pkg/services/team/`)
   - Team creation, membership, and team-level permissions

3. **Group Synchronization** (`pkg/services/ldap/` + `pkg/services/authn/clients/`)
   - Maps IdP groups (LDAP, OAuth, SAML, Auth Proxy) to Grafana teams

4. **Identity Sync Pipeline** (`pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/`)
   - User sync, org role sync, and RBAC permission sync hooks
   - Handles team membership assignment during authentication

5. **Folder Permission Inheritance** (`pkg/services/dashboards/` + `pkg/services/folder/`)
   - Inherited scopes from parent folders to child dashboards

6. **RBAC Permission Evaluation** (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/` + `pkg/services/authz/rbac/`)
   - Fine-grained action-scope permission checking
   - Team membership resolution and role-based access control

The answer file includes detailed information about 33 key symbols and the complete permission evaluation chain, along with a narrative explanation of how the system works end-to-end.

  "text": "The organization/team/user permission hierarchy in Grafana is implemented across multiple interdependent services. Here's how they work together:\n\n## OrgUser Role Assignment\nThe core organization user management is defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` with the `OrgUser` struct representing an organization membership record. The assignment process is handled through:\n- `pkg/services/org/orgimpl/store.go` - Contains `InsertOrgUser`, `AddOrgUser`, and `UpdateOrgUser` functions that manage org user database operations\n- `pkg/api/org_users.go` - HTTP API endpoints for managing organization users and their roles\n\n## Team Management and Sync\nTeam functionality is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/team/model.go` - Defines `Team` and `TeamMember` structures\n- `pkg/services/team/teamimpl/team.go` - Team service implementation\n- `pkg/services/team/teamimpl/store.go` - Database operations including `AddOrUpdateTeamMemberHook` and `RemoveTeamMemberHook` for team membership changes\n- `pkg/services/team/teamapi/team_members.go` - HTTP API for team member management with support for both manual and LDAP-synced members\n\n## Team/Group Synchronization from Identity Providers\nThe team synchronization from external identity providers is handled through:\n- `pkg/services/ldap/ldap_groups.go` - Defines the `Groups` interface with `GetTeams()` method that maps LDAP groups to Grafana teams\n- `pkg/services/ldap/ldap.go` - LDAP user and group retrieval logic\n- `pkg/services/ldap/api/service.go` - API endpoint for syncing users with LDAP, including team membership\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/ldap.go` - LDAP authentication client that populates `Identity.Groups` from LDAP group memberships\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/oauth.go` - OAuth2 client that extracts groups from OAuth2 tokens\n- `pkg/services/authn/clients/proxy.go` - Auth proxy client that reads groups from proxy headers\n\n## Identity Synchronization Hooks\nThe identity synchronization pipeline manages both user and team sync:\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/user_sync.go` - `SyncUserHook` and `FetchSyncedUserHook` coordinate user data sync and populate teams\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/org_sync.go` - `SyncOrgRolesHook` handles organization role assignment from identity providers\n- `pkg/services/authn/authnimpl/sync/rbac_sync.go` - `SyncPermissionsHook` fetches and applies RBAC permissions\n- `pkg/services/authn/identity.go` - Core `Identity` struct with `Groups` field (IdP groups) and `Teams` field (assigned Grafana teams)\n- `pkg/services/authn/authn.go` - `ClientParams` struct defines `SyncTeams` and `SyncOrgRoles` flags\n\n## Folder Permission Inheritance\nFolder-level permissions are inherited by dashboards within them:\n- `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - `GetInheritedScopes()` function retrieves inherited permission scopes from parent folders\n- `pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go` - Folder service that checks inherited permissions during folder access\n- `pkg/api/folder.go` - HTTP API for folder management with permission evaluation\n- `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` - Folder permission endpoints\n\n## RBAC Fine-Grained Permission Evaluation\nThe RBAC permission evaluation engine is implemented in:\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` - `AccessControl` interface for permission checks and `Service` interface for role management\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` - `Evaluator` type that constructs permission evaluation expressions with AND/OR logic\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/access_control.go` - `Evaluate()` method performs actual permission checks against user permissions\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` - `Permission` (role-action-scope binding) and `ResourcePermission` models\n- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` - `SetUserResourcePermission()` and team/role permission management\n- `pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go` - `getUserTeams()` retrieves teams for a user and their inherited permissions\n- `pkg/services/authz/rbac/store/queries.go` - Low-level SQL queries for permission resolution\n- `pkg/registry/apis/iam/resourcepermission/sql.go` - Resource permission SQL backend\n\n## Permission Flow\nWhen a user authenticates:\n1. Identity provider groups are extracted into `Identity.Groups`\n2. `SyncUserHook` creates/updates the user and `FetchSyncedUserHook` populates `Identity.Teams` with assigned teams\n3. `SyncOrgRolesHook` assigns org-level roles from IdP org role mappings\n4. `SyncPermissionsHook` loads user permissions via RBAC service\n5. `AccessControl.Evaluate()` checks permissions using action-scope pairs\n6. For folders/dashboards, `GetInheritedScopes()` includes parent folder permissions\n7. RBAC service resolves team memberships and applies role-based permissions\n\nThis hierarchy ensures that users inherit permissions through organizations, teams, and folder inheritance, while RBAC provides fine-grained control over individual actions on resources."
}
